name: v05-async-job-poller
description: v0.6 wait-inside-primitives Phase 2 demo — sequential forEach with a wait inside the iteration body. The handler iterates over a list of job IDs from `req.body.jobs`, simulates polling each job (recording a result), then waits 100ms before the next iteration so an external service isn't hammered. The wait inside the iteration body is the load-bearing test of v0.6 Phase 2: each iteration's wait fires `WaitDispatchRequest` from inside the forEach iteration scope, the runner persists the iteration cursor to `node_runs.iteration_context`, defers the run, then on `dispatchDeferred` re-entry the forEach skips iterations [0..N-1] (using the cached `completedResults`) and resumes iteration N at innerStepIndex 1 (the wait), which flips to satisfied because the timer has fired. Real-world analogue: bulk-notify with a per-recipient throttle, async-job poller cycling through multiple jobs, batch reconciliation with rate-limited inner steps. Each iteration round-trips via dispatchDeferred so 3 jobs ≈ 300ms wall-clock; smoke validates the first 202 + Location header (full round-trip is verified in the vitest integration suite).

inputs.expression: ({ ok: true, totalJobs: ctx.state['process-jobs'].length, results: ctx.state['process-jobs'] })
